claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£0.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child is not in local authority care
Child in hospital for 13 weeks but claimant regularly spends on child
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine whether you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with the basics:

**How many children are you applying for Child Benefit for?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit. I have one child - my daughter Alex, who is 6 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's helpful to know you have one daughter, Alex, who is 6 years old.

Before I can assess your eligibility, I need to understand your living situation. Let me ask:

**Do you live with Alex, or does she live with someone else?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me - she lives with her dad.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that. Since Alex lives with her dad, I need to understand the arrangements a bit better.

**Does Alex's dad currently claim Child Benefit for her, or are you aware of whether anyone is claiming Child Benefit for her?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, her dad isn't claiming Child Benefit for her. Nobody else is claiming it as far as I know.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: That's helpful to know. Before I can make a final assessment, I need to check the specific rules about Child Benefit eligibility when a child doesn't live with the person claiming. Let me review the government guidance on this.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the government guidance on Child Benefit eligibility. I can now see that since Alex lives with her dad rather than with you, there are specific rules that apply.

According to the rules, you can potentially still claim Child Benefit for Alex, but I need to ask you one more important question:

**Are you currently making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (such as towards her food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation)? If so, how much are you contributing, and how often?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, I'm not currently contributing financially towards Alex's upkeep at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I now have all the details I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on the government guidance, let me explain the situation:

According to the rules, when a child doesn't live with you, you can initially claim Child Benefit for up to 8 weeks after the child goes to live with someone else, provided nobody else is claiming. However, to continue claiming beyond 8 weeks, you would need to be making financial contributions towards the child's upkeep (such as towards food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation) in an amount at least equal to the Child Benefit payment itself.

Since you've indicated that you are not currently making any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep, this affects your eligibility.

**To clarify: Has Alex been living with her dad for more or less than 8 weeks?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: She's been living with him for quite a while now - it's been a couple of months at least.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that clarification. I now have all the information I need to make a final assessment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Summary

**You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time.**

Here's why:

- Alex has been living with her dad for a couple of months, which is beyond the initial 8-week period
- You are not currently making financial contributions towards her upkeep
- Child Benefit rules state that after the first 8 weeks of a child living elsewhere, you can only continue to claim if you're contributing financially towards their care (in an amount at least equal to the Child Benefit payment)

**However, you could become eligible if:**
- You start making regular financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (such as towards her food, clothes, pocket money, or accommodation) in an amount at least equal to the Child Benefit payment, or
- Alex comes to live with you

If you have any questions or if your circumstances change, you can contact the Child Benefit Office for further advice.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and lives with her dad, not with you. According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you can claim for up to 8 weeks after a child goes to live with someone else (provided nobody else is claiming). However, to continue claiming beyond 8 weeks, you must be making financial contributions towards the child's upkeep in an amount at least equal to the Child Benefit payment. Since Alex has been living with her dad for a couple of months (which is beyond the 8-week period) and you are not currently making any financial contributions towards her upkeep, you do not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time. She has been living with her dad for longer than 8 weeks, and you are not making financial contributions towards her upkeep. To become eligible again, you would need to either start making regular financial contributions towards Alex's care (in an amount at least equal to the Child Benefit payment) or have her come to live with you."

However, to continue claiming beyond 8 weeks, you must be making financial contributions towards the child's upkeep in an amount at least equal to the Child Benefit payment. Since Alex has been living with her dad for a couple of months (which is beyond the 8-week period) and you are not currently making any financial contributions towards her upkeep, you do not meet the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: You are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time. She has been living with her dad for longer than 8 weeks, and you are not making financial contributions towards her upkeep. To become eligible again, you would need to either start making regular financial contributions towards Alex's care (in an amount at least equal to the Child Benefit payment) or have her come to live with you."
